response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a technically capable individual contributor with broad full-stack and distributed-systems experience, but the profile is fundamentally misaligned with a CTO role at this level. There is no evidence of engineering leadership, team management, or organizational scaling — the role's hardest requirements. His agentic AI exposure is limited to a few months at his current position and is described in vague, tooling-oriented terms rather than production systems at scale. Fintech experience exists but only as an IC in adjacent domains, not as an architect of credit, risk, or compliance infrastructure. The candidate is several career stages away from the executive mandate this position demands.
strengths: Strong distributed-systems and high-throughput backend experience (Sociomantic: millions of HTTP requests/second, low-latency async I/O with DLang)
Recent hands-on exposure to agentic AI infrastructure and MCP framework tooling at re:cinq, showing directional alignment with the space
Demonstrated versatility across multiple languages, platforms, and infrastructure layers (TypeScript, Node.js, Docker, Kubernetes, microservices)
concerns: Zero evidence of people management or engineering leadership — no team scaling, no org design, no hiring track record, no mention of direct reports at any point in career
Agentic AI experience is nascent (months old), described at a tooling/framework level, with no evidence of production multi-agent systems operating in high-stakes or regulated contexts
No CTO-level responsibilities — no board communication, no technical strategy ownership, no governance of outsourced engineering, no cross-functional executive engagement
